name: Cream Cheese Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend the butter and cream cheese until smooth.
2. Beat in the flour until a dough forms.
3. Chill the dough overnight.
4. Drain the preserves, removing as much liquid as possible while keeping fruit pieces intact.
5. Roll out pastry on a floured baking sheet to form 2-inch rounds.
6. (Save scraps, roll into a ball, chill, and reroll.)
7. Place a dab of preserves on each round.
8. Fold the dough into crescents and seal edges tightly.
9. Brush tops with egg white mixed with water.
10. Dip tops in a mixture of ground nuts and sugar.
11. Arrange cookies close together on an ungreased cookie sheet.
12. Bake at 325°F (163°C) until just starting to brown, about 15 minutes. Watch carefully as they burn easily.
13. Remove immediately and cool on wire racks. Pack tightly in an airtight container. These freeze well but are best consumed within a few days at room temperature.
(Note: The original recipe included additional information such as nutritional data, notes, and personal comments from the chef, which have been omitted per the instructions.)
